responsibility: Accept raw OCPP WebSocket connections at /ocpp/:chargePointId and validate charger identity, protocol, and lifecycle messages.
Parse OCPP CALL/CALLRESULT/CALLERROR frames, then map BootNotification, Heartbeat, StatusNotification, MeterValues, Authorize, StartTransaction, and StopTransaction into CPMS domain events.
Queue operator commands such as RemoteStartTransaction, RemoteStopTransaction, Reset, UnlockConnector, ChangeAvailability, and ChangeConfiguration.
Expose normalized charge point, session, meter, alert, command, tariff, user, and report data to the frontend.
Replace current JSON storage with database tables for chargers, connectors, sessions, meter values, idTags, commands, events, tariffs, and audit logs.
Show station health, OCPP connection state, protocol events, command queue, remote controls, sessions, energy, revenue, and faults.
